message:
feat(core): wire quotient filter into direct-simple DELETE/UPDATE (IMPL-9a complete)

Follow-up to 7365e970 (scaffolding). The per-table QuotientFilter is now
consulted before the B-tree descent in direct-simple UPDATE/DELETE and
maintained on every successful direct-simple INSERT/DELETE, with correct
invalidation at transaction boundaries.

Sites wired (crates/fsqlite-core/src/connection.rs):
- `execute_prepared_direct_simple_update_with_cursor`: calls
  `qf_maybe_short_circuit_for_rowid` before `cursor.table_move_to`. A
  positive result returns Ok((0, false)) without touching the B-tree.
- `execute_prepared_direct_simple_delete_with_cursor`: same consultation
  hook; also calls `qf_record_delete` after a successful `cursor.delete`.
  (This inner helper gains `&self` + `root_page` so it can drive both
  the filter and the maintenance update; the two outer callers are
  updated to pass them through.)
- `execute_prepared_direct_simple_insert_with_cursor`: calls
  `qf_record_insert` after the row has been appended/inserted into the
  B-tree (past all conflict-action branches).
- `execute_commit_with_cx`: on successful commit, calls
  `clear_qf_txn_modifications` so a subsequent transaction's rollback
  cannot wrongly invalidate filters whose writes are durably committed.
- `execute_commit_with_cx` (forced-rollback branch on vtab sync
  failure): calls `invalidate_qfs_on_rollback`.
- `execute_rollback_with_cx` (full ROLLBACK path): calls
  `invalidate_qfs_on_rollback`.
- `execute_rollback_with_cx` (ROLLBACK TO SAVEPOINT path): calls
  `invalidate_qfs_on_rollback`. Conservative — any filter mutated
  within the enclosing txn is reseeded on next consult.

Rollback semantics (correctness invariant):
- `qf_record_insert` / `qf_record_delete` mark the per-filter
  `modified_in_txn` flag only when `self.in_transaction.get()` is
  true. On ROLLBACK, `invalidate_qfs_on_rollback` resets every
  entry with `modified_in_txn=true` to the unbuilt state so the
  next consultation reseeds from the post-rollback committed B-tree.
  This is what eliminates the false-negative ("filter says absent but
  row is in fact present in committed state") failure mode.
- False-positives are tolerated (`contains()==true` just falls
  through to the normal descent). False-negatives are fatal and
  cannot occur: we only short-circuit once the filter is `built` AND
  `contains(hash)==false`, and we only mark it `built` after a full
  scan of the committed B-tree (or a rollback-forced reseed).

Helpers unwired:
- `qf_invalidate_root` (DDL invalidation). The DROP TABLE / ALTER
  TABLE root-page plumbing is non-trivial and the filter can be
  safely (if wastefully) left behind — consultation only targets the
  OLD root page until its entry is next touched. Retained under
  `#[allow(dead_code)]` with the rationale documented inline.

`#[allow(dead_code)]` removed from every helper now invoked, from the
`QuotientFilterEntry` struct, from the `QUOTIENT_FILTER_BUILD_ROW_CAP`
constant, and from the `Connection::quotient_filters` /
`quotient_filter_short_circuits` fields. The wiring-status comment
above the helpers is updated to list every consumption site.

Tests (`connection::pager_routing_tests`):
1. `test_quotient_filter_short_circuits_absent_rowids_on_delete`:
   inserts 100 rowids in [0, 100), then issues 100 DELETEs in
   [10_000, 10_100) inside an explicit transaction. Asserts that
   ≥ 90 of them short-circuit via the QF (slack reserved for the
   one-shot build scan on first consultation).
2. `test_quotient_filter_delete_then_redelete_short_circuits`:
   after INSERT + direct-simple DELETE of a rowid, a second
   DELETE of the same rowid must short-circuit (the filter's
   `qf_record_delete` maintenance worked).
3. `test_quotient_filter_rollback_forces_reseed`: INSERT under a
   txn, ROLLBACK, and assert that `modified_in_txn` is cleared on
   every filter entry. A post-rollback DELETE of the never-
   committed rowid must return 0 rows (committed state is empty)
   and must not corrupt the B-tree.

All three QF tests pass. Existing `test_direct_simple_update_delete_
fast_path_{executes_and_is_correct,skipped_when_index_present}` remain
green. `cargo clippy -p fsqlite-core` is clean; `cargo fmt` is clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
